summary:
Vanishing testes syndrome involves non-malignant, progressive atrophy typically linked to conditions such as Sertoli-cell-only syndrome, making surgical intervention unnecessary. Testis-sparing approaches are only justified when there’s clear evidence of benign lesions without concerning features or progression risk; thus, routine surgical resection isn't required based on histology alone.  

(Note: This summary focuses strictly on clinically significant aspects related directly to the query about whether surgical treatment should be performed.)

First sentence must establish what the condition entails histologically and why it doesn’t require surgery. Second sentence addresses how surgical decisions apply specifically under this context using established guidelines.

Final output will reflect just those core elements tied directly to the medical rationale behind avoiding operative intervention.
